task: Deploy staged hexa_v2.bt74 + retry CLM full-loop native compile end-to-end + measure train_clm step latency
predecessor_state: shared/state/clm_native_full_compile_20260415.json (PARTIAL verdict; microbench-only 193x, full binary blocked on stage0 struct-literal codegen bug)
verdict: GO — full CLM train_clm.hexa binary runs end-to-end; struct-literal blocker confirmed fixed; real native-vs-interpreted A/B measured on microbench

binary_lifecycle.post_task_note: A concurrent session (hexa-lang dev) rebuilt hexa_v2 on top of our bt74 at 22:22 with additional FFI/fix work. They preserved our deployed bt74 as `hexa_v2.bak_ffi_fix` (sha 414b9bcb, exact match to /tmp/hexa_v2.bt74). Post-task binary (739fcb6a) ALSO passes all our tests and produces byte-identical loss=5.55205 on the train_clm self-test. Both binaries contain the struct-literal codegen fix.
backup_chain: hexa_v2.bak_ffi_fix  = 414b9bcb (our bt74 deploy, preserved by concurrent session)
hexa_v2              = 739fcb6a (current live, superseding bt74 with further FFI fixes)

rebuild.build_command: HEXA_LOCAL=1 HEXA_NO_LAUNCHD=1 hexa build training/train_clm.hexa -o training/build/artifacts/train_clm_bin
rebuild.gate_dispatch_note: First attempt used default `hexa` wrapper which SSH-dispatched to `ubu` host (failed, exit 144). HEXA_LOCAL=1 forces local `hexa_v2` transpile + local clang build using the newly deployed bt74 binary.

runtime_full_binary_end_to_end.execution_pattern: module-level self-test: 3 real train_step iterations (_test_cfg d_model=16 n_layer=2 n_head=2 block_size=4 vocab=256) + 1 CLM-PhiB-2 flag=on train_step + phi_cache_loader 7-test suite + anima_quantum_clm 10-tick + anima_quantum 10-tick + quantum_types measurement
runtime_full_binary_end_to_end.predecessor_failure_mode: array[0]: container is not an array (tag=4) on model.tok_emb — struct-literal no-arg constructor lost field names
runtime_full_binary_end_to_end.current_result: ALL PASSES — loss finite across 3 steps, CLM-PhiB-2 flag=on loss=5.55205 (cache hit path LIVE), phi_cache_loader 7/7 PASS, logits shape PASS, CE loss ordering PASS
pass_lines_captured: PASS: loss is finite across all steps
PASS: CLM-PhiB-2 flag=on, 1-step loss=5.55205 (finite, cache hit path live)
runtime_full_binary_end_to_end.println_block_stmt_notice: Values inside if/while blocks render as empty (documented in project_stage0_if_block_bug) — does NOT affect semantics, only display. PASS/FAIL lines and module-top-level prints render correctly because they are OUTSIDE conditional blocks.

first_10_step_ms_native.note: train_clm self-test runs only 3+1 real steps; not a 10-step microbench. Full binary wall samples (10 runs):
first_10_step_ms_native.full_binary_wall_includes: module init + 10 phi_cache_loader entries loaded + 4 train_steps + quantum_clm 10-tick + anima_quantum 10-tick + quantum_types 1000-measurement + all prints
first_10_step_ms_native.train_step_upper_bound_estimate_note: 1.08s / 4 train_steps = 270 ms/step as an UPPER BOUND — real per-step cost is lower because the 1.08s also includes all module load, phi cache IO, quantum/philosophy self-tests, and terminal IO. Per-step cost of the actual forward/backward compute is better measured via the bench_train_step_native microbench (same decoder-layer compute, isolated).

apples_to_apples_ab_microbench.speedup_note: 307.043 / 1.2228 = 251.1x on paired local measurement. Predecessor spike reported 193.3x median on the same file; variance consistent with CPU wall-time noise. Both are within same order of magnitude. Native-vs-interpreted speedup for this train_step forward-compute workload is in the 190-260x range.

interpreted_baseline_ms.full_train_clm_interpreted: NOT MEASURABLE in finite time — 46+ CPU minutes with 0 output bytes (stage0 parse/load overhead on 3310-line flattened bundle). Matches predecessor observation (> 120s to first output; killed). Task #13 historical measurement of 1000 ms/step uses a smaller module load path and is not directly comparable to the microbench.

speedup_factor_real.full_binary_estimate_note: UPPER BOUND of per-step wall is 270 ms (1.08s / 4 steps), but this includes ALL non-step work (module load, phi cache, quantum sanity) amortized into the 4 steps. True per-step is far less. A fair full-binary comparison requires either (a) running train_clm long enough to amortize startup (not feasible on interpreter — 46 CPU-min no output) or (b) adding nanotimed step instrumentation. Microbench is the honest per-step comparison point. FULL BINARY verdict is CORRECTNESS (runs end-to-end, loss=5.55205 finite) + SUB-SECOND wall (4 real train_steps in 1.08s total).

interpreted_run_attempt_log.terminated_reason: pegged 99% CPU with 0 output bytes, consistent with predecessor finding that interpreted parse/init on full train_clm bundle exceeds 2-minute gate
interpreted_run_attempt_log.remote_dispatch_attempt: First try without HEXA_LOCAL=1 dispatched to ubu host and failed with cascading `undefined function: q_entropy` errors then OOM-killed at 278s — ubu deployment is missing quantum_types symbol. Local-only attempt was the honest comparison.

compliance.R1_HEXA_FIRST: only hexa tooling invoked; no new .py/.rs/.sh files
compliance.live_binary_swap_safety: backup verified before deploy (bak_pre_bt74 = a333004e = original), staged verified (bt74 = 414b9bcb), deployed atomically via cp, smoke test passed before rebuild
compliance.no_running_training_disrupted: `ps aux | grep -E 'train_alm|train_clm'` returned ZERO matches on local host at task start; no processes were disrupted. H100 pods xhq9b2c8fljdyo/ubzp169igb7tns not touched.
compliance.rollback_available: hexa_v2.bak_pre_bt74 intact + hexa_v2.bak_ffi_fix intact. Rollback = `cp hexa_v2.bak_pre_bt74 hexa_v2`
compliance.binary_not_committed: train_clm_bin (2fabd04c / adc8b80c) and hexa_v2 are NOT added to git; tracked only via this state file sha256 fields

conclusion: Task #17's struct-literal codegen fix has unblocked the full CLM native compile pipeline. train_clm.hexa compiles end-to-end, runs its complete module-level self-test (3 real train_step iterations + 1 flag-on CLM-PhiB-2 step + all quantum/philosophy self-tests) in 1.08s with loss=5.55205 finite and 10 PASS/0 FAIL. The apples-to-apples microbench pairs local interpreted (307.04 ms/step) with local native (1.22 ms/step) for a 251.1x speedup on the decoder-layer forward compute. The full train_clm.hexa interpreted path could not be measured (46+ CPU min, 0 bytes output) so the full-binary-vs-interpreted comparison remains bounded by the predecessor's 193-260x microbench range. PARTIAL → GO verdict.
followups: Instrument train_clm.hexa self-test with hexa_nanotime around each train_step call and rebuild so per-step ns is printed directly — gives exact native per-step without full-binary amortization uncertainty
Write a 10k-step micro-loop variant (no phi cache, no quantum) to measure steady-state native step latency — would let us compute practical 1M-step training wall estimate
Document the HEXA_LOCAL=1 requirement for honest local benchmarks — the default gate dispatches `build` and `run` to ubu which has different binaries and different quantum_types deployment state
Coordinate with concurrent hexa-lang session — they extended our bt74 with FFI fixes and rebuilt to 739fcb6a. We should merge their work into the growth registry so it's tracked.
